title: The Little Mouse Who Planted Kindness
moral: Every small act of kindness is like a seed — you never know how big and beautiful it will grow.
moralZh: 每一个小小的善举都像一颗种子——你永远不知道它会长得多么高大美丽。

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: At the edge of Whispering Woods, in a tiny house made of a walnut shell, lived a little mouse named Milo. Milo was small — smaller than a pinecone — but his heart was bigger than the sky.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Every morning, Milo would wake up and ask himself the same question: "Who can I help today?"

paragraphs.speaker: Mama Mouse
paragraphs.text: "Milo, you're always running off to do things for others. Don't you ever want to rest?"

paragraphs.speaker: Milo
paragraphs.text: "Resting is nice, Mama. But helping feels even better! Besides, kindness is like planting seeds. The more you plant, the more grows!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Milo scampered through the woods. Near the old stone wall, he saw Mrs. Squirrel staring sadly at her flower patch. The flowers were drooping and wilting in the hot sun.

paragraphs.speaker: Mrs. Squirrel
paragraphs.text: "Oh dear, my beautiful flowers. The stream is too far, and my arms are too tired to carry water..."

paragraphs.speaker: Milo
paragraphs.text: "Don't worry, Mrs. Squirrel! I'll help!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Milo found a large leaf, folded it into a cup, and made trip after trip to the stream. Each time, he carried just a few drops — but after many trips, the flowers perked up and smiled at the sun.

paragraphs.speaker: Mrs. Squirrel
paragraphs.text: "Milo, you saved my garden! Thank you so much!"

paragraphs.speaker: Milo
paragraphs.text: "It was nothing! A garden is happiest when it has friends to care for it."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Further along the path, Milo found Mr. Owl sitting under a tree, looking very confused. His round glasses were missing.

paragraphs.speaker: Mr. Owl
paragraphs.text: "Hootie-hoo... without my glasses, I can't read my books. Everything is just a fuzzy blur!"

paragraphs.speaker: Milo
paragraphs.text: "I'll find them for you, Mr. Owl! Where were you reading last?"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Milo searched high and low. He looked under bushes, inside hollow logs, and behind rocks. Finally, tucked between two roots of the old oak tree, he spotted the shiny glasses.

paragraphs.speaker: Milo
paragraphs.text: "Found them! Here you go, Mr. Owl!"

paragraphs.speaker: Mr. Owl
paragraphs.text: "Hootie-ray! Milo, you have the keenest eyes in the whole forest! How can I ever repay you?"

paragraphs.speaker: Milo
paragraphs.text: "Just read me a story sometime! That would be repayment enough."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Near the berry patch, Milo spotted Benny the hedgehog sitting alone. Benny was very shy and always curled into a ball when anyone came near.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Milo didn't say a word. He quietly picked a handful of the juiciest berries and placed them on a leaf next to Benny, then scurried away.

paragraphs.speaker: Benny
paragraphs.text: "Wait — who left these?"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Benny looked around and saw a tiny mouse tail disappear behind a bush. He smiled and uncurled just a little bit.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Days passed. Milo continued his secret kindness missions — mending a bird's nest, clearing a blocked path, leaving acorns for a tired traveler. He never asked for thanks. He never expected anything in return.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Then one afternoon, a big storm rolled over Whispering Woods. The wind howled, rain poured, and Milo's little walnut house was swept off its branch and smashed into pieces.

paragraphs.speaker: Milo
paragraphs.text: "Oh no! My house! Where will I sleep tonight?"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Milo was wet, cold, and his paw was bruised from the fall. He sat under a mushroom, shivering. But word spread quickly through the forest.

paragraphs.speaker: Mrs. Squirrel
paragraphs.text: "Milo! I heard what happened! Come, stay in my den tonight. I've made a warm bed of moss just for you."

paragraphs.speaker: Mr. Owl
paragraphs.text: "Hootie-hoo! And I'll bring over some hot berry tea for that paw!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Then came Benny the hedgehog, rolling a big pile of soft leaves.

paragraphs.speaker: Benny
paragraphs.text: "Milo... you always left me berries when I was lonely and too shy to ask. Now it's my turn to help you. These leaves will keep you warm all night."

paragraphs.speaker: Milo
paragraphs.text: "Benny! You uncurled! I'm so happy to see you!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The next morning, Milo woke up to a wonderful surprise. All the animals of Whispering Woods had gathered outside Mrs. Squirrel's den. They had brought twigs, leaves, moss, and stones — everything needed to build a new house.

paragraphs.speaker: Mr. Owl
paragraphs.text: "We couldn't let our little helper sleep in a mushroom forever! Everyone chipped in."

paragraphs.speaker: Milo
paragraphs.text: "B-but... I never asked for anything..."

paragraphs.speaker: Benny
paragraphs.text: "That's why you deserve it the most, Milo. Kindness doesn't ask — it gives. And today, we're giving back to you."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: By sunset, Milo had a brand-new home — bigger and cozier than before. Mrs. Squirrel had woven flower vines around the entrance. Mr. Owl had placed a tiny bookshelf inside. And Benny had left a fresh pile of berries by the doorstep.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Milo stood at the door of his new house, looking at his friends. His heart swelled with warmth.

paragraphs.speaker: Milo
paragraphs.text: "I started by planting little seeds of kindness... and today, I harvested a garden of friendship. Thank you, everyone!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And so, Milo the mouse learned that kindness really is like planting seeds. You may not see them grow right away. But one day, when you least expect it, the whole world blossoms around you.
